target.title: feat(agents): add top-level subsystem logger
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds `src/agents/log.ts` exporting a `SubsystemLogger` for the `"agents"` subsystem
- Follows the existing pattern of `src/agents/cli-runner/log.ts` and `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/logger.ts`
- Wires `agent-command.ts` as the first caller via `agentsLog.child("agent-command")` — identical runtime behavior to the previous `createSubsystemLogger("agents/agent-command")` call

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `src/agents/log.ts` adds a shared subsystem logger for the agents module so consumers can call `log.child(...)` instead of importing `createSubsystemLogger` directly.

Real environment tested: Node v24.15.0, tsx v4.22.3, openclaw repo on commit e8416d4a36 (Windows 11 / PowerShell)

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```
node_modules/.bin/tsx --eval "
import { log } from './src/agents/log.ts';
console.log('subsystem:', log.subsystem);
const child = log.child('agent-command');
console.log('child subsystem:', child.subsystem);
console.log('isEnabled(info):', log.isEnabled('info'));
"
```

Evidence after fix:
```
subsystem: agents
child subsystem: agents/agent-command
isEnabled(info): true
```

Observed result after fix: `log.subsystem` is `"agents"`, `log.child("agent-command").subsystem` is `"agents/agent-command"` — same string the previous direct `createSubsystemLogger("agents/agent-command")` call produced. No behavior change.

What was not tested: Other callers that may eventually import from `src/agents/log.ts`; no existing tests changed.

## Verification

CI run on head `e8416d4a36`: `build-artifacts` ✅ `check-dependencies` ✅ `check-prod-types` ✅ `checks-node-agentic-agents` ✅
target.author: caulslorenzo1-png
